condense_history.description: Condense one batch of old history lines (chat, dungeon log, or battle log) into a compact summary, keeping only what could matter later

run_chronicle.description: The post-run chronicle: one streamed story beat condensing the whole run, stamped with its run number (persisted to the run's history row)
run_chronicle.prompt_template:
You are the chronicler of a fantasy monster-hunting saga, writing the entry for an expedition that just ended. These entries ARE the saga - each run of the dungeon becomes one story beat in the record.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- The run being chronicled ---
Run number {run_number}, ending in {result_word} for the party.

--- The party ---
{party_summary}

--- The goal ---
{goal_line}

--- Companions ---
{companions_line}

--- Everything that happened, oldest first ---
{dungeon_log}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Write the chronicle entry: ONE flowing paragraph of 4-6 sentences in plain past tense, condensing this run into the story it was. Include: how the expedition began and what it sought, the moments that mattered (use the log - real names, real events), the goal's fate, who was met, won over, or lost, and how it ended. Open with 'Run {run_number}:' exactly. Vivid but factual - never invent anything the log does not say. No headers, no lists.

Response:
